title: gh-128067: Add newline to new REPL output when needed to avoid chopping characters
author: devdanzin
body:
This PR attempts to fix #128067 (and #128809) by capturing REPL output into a `StringIO`, checking whether it ends in a newline and adding one if necessary. Works on Windows and Linux.

Before:
```python
>>> print('abcdefg' * 250 + 'Z', end=' ')

>>> bcdefgabcdefgabcdefgabcdefgabcdefgabcdefgabcdefgabcdefgabcdefgabcdefgabcdefgabcdefgabcdefgabcdefgabcdefgabcdefgabcdefgZ
```
After:
```python
>>> print('abcdefg' * 250 + 'Z', end=' ')

>>>
```

Feel free to suggest improvements. If this approach doesn't make sense, I have no great attachment to this PR.

<!-- gh-issue-number: gh-128067 -->
* Issue: gh-128067
<!-- /gh-issue-number -->

comments.author: devdanzin
comments.body: This approach has many downsides and corner cases, closing to avoid wasting reviewer's time.
